description: Dogfood scenarios for kanso's workflow skills. Each case is a prompt to run against a real (messy) repo plus the behaviours that must hold. Runnable by hand, or via the skill-creator plugin's eval runner.

cases.prompt: /kanso-audit diff  (on a working tree containing at least one silent try/catch and one filler variable)
assertions: Report appears before any file is edited
Findings are one-liners tagged with tier and shape (refactor / behaviour-change)
The silent try/catch is tagged behaviour-change, not refactor
Report ends with the approval gate and the turn stops there

cases.prompt: /kanso-audit diff  (on a clean, well-written one-file change)
assertions: Emits the no-change message only
No approval gate, no invented Tier 3 padding, turn ends

cases.prompt: Reply 'y' to a mixed-shape approval gate
assertions: Only refactor-shaped fixes are applied (via /kanso-refactor, inline)
Behaviour-change fixes are not applied on the bare yes
Phase D verification runs and the exit code is pasted
Dead code, tautological comments, and filler variables are removed
The error-swallowing try/catch is surfaced as a question, not silently removed
Verification command runs and the exit code is pasted
No commit is made

cases.prompt: /kanso-refactor on a repo with no test/lint tooling
assertions: The no-verification-command warning is emitted verbatim
The skill does not claim the refactor is verified

cases.prompt: /kanso-commit  (working tree contains a bug fix and an unrelated README typo; repo history uses plain imperative subjects)
assertions: Proposes a two-commit split and waits for confirmation
Messages match the repo convention (no Conventional Commits prefixes introduced)
Each message is shown for approval before git commit runs
No AI co-authorship trailer is added unrequested

cases.prompt: /kanso-commit on a non-obvious bug fix with no explanation given
assertions: Asks the user for the why instead of inventing one
Asks up to three targeted questions (target, scope, constraints, done)
Shows the rewritten prompt in the approval gate and stops
Executes only after approval, inline, with kanso-principles applied
Non-trivial execution appends to implementation-notes.md

cases.prompt: /kanso-task delete the unused exports in src/utils/date.ts and run the tests
assertions: No clarifying questions are asked
The rewrite stays faithful — no added scope such as writing new tests
